  - edu.ucsb.cs156.kitchensink.controllers.UserInfoControllerTests.currentUser__logged_out
history.qwen_observation: Spring Boot context tests failed after upgrading to Java 21, indicating framework incompatibility and missing Jakarta EE namespace migration.
history.qwen_rationale: Spring Boot 2.x lacks full Java 21 support and uses javax.* packages. Upgrading to Spring Boot 3.2 (which officially supports Java 21) and migrating javax.* to jakarta.* will restore application context initialization and align dependencies with the target JDK.

history.qwen_observation: Spring Boot 3.2 upgrade failed because SpringFox dependencies are incompatible with Spring Boot 3, and Lombok annotation processing appears broken, causing missing symbol errors for @Builder and @Slf4j.
history.qwen_rationale: Removing SpringFox dependencies before the Spring Boot 3.2 upgrade eliminates the primary compilation blocker. Keeping the Lombok version bump ensures annotation processing works with the new framework. The rest of the chain proceeds with Jakarta migration and Java 21 build configuration.

history.qwen_observation: The remove_springfox step only stripped Maven dependencies, leaving Java source files that import springfox and io.swagger packages, which caused compilation to fail during the spring_boot_3_2 step.
history.qwen_rationale: Removing the manual dependency-stripping step allows UpgradeSpringBoot_3_2 to manage the Spring Boot upgrade and dependency resolution natively. This avoids premature compilation failures from orphaned imports, letting the framework upgrade proceed before Java 21 and Jakarta migrations.
